description: Cost-saving guardrails to prevent recurrence of FinOps findings, expressed as Azure Policy suggestions. 'policy_definition_id' is the built-in definition GUID under /providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyDefinitions/ where one exists; entries marked 'custom' need a custom definition (starter logic in 'custom_policy_hint'). Assign at management-group or subscription scope; start with Audit effects, move to Deny/Modify once exemptions are understood.

guardrails.title: Require cost-allocation tags on resource groups
guardrails.policy: Require a tag on resource groups (built-in)
guardrails.notes: Assign once per required tag (CostCenter, Owner, Environment, Application). Blocks untraceable spend at creation time.

guardrails.title: Inherit cost-allocation tags from resource group
guardrails.policy: Inherit a tag from the resource group if missing (built-in)
guardrails.notes: Auto-remediates missing tags on child resources so cost reports stay complete without developer friction. Needs a managed identity on the assignment for remediation.

guardrails.title: Restrict VM sizes to an approved, cost-vetted list
guardrails.notes: Prevents oversized/GPU/premium SKUs outside approved families. Maintain separate allowed lists per environment (dev lists exclude production-class SKUs).

guardrails.title: Restrict deployments to approved (lower-cost) regions
guardrails.notes: Region price differences of 10-30% are common; also reduces egress by keeping workloads co-located.

guardrails.title: Block expensive or unsanctioned resource types
guardrails.notes: Typical candidates in non-production scopes: ExpressRoute circuits, premium Bastion SKUs, dedicated hosts, HSM. Pair with an exemption process.

guardrails.title: Restrict storage accounts to cost-appropriate SKUs
guardrails.notes: Block premium/GZRS SKUs in scopes that don't need them; dev/test rarely needs anything beyond Standard_LRS.

guardrails.title: Audit unattached managed disks and orphaned public IPs
guardrails.custom_policy_hint: Audit policy over Microsoft.Compute/disks where properties.diskState equals 'Unattached', and Microsoft.Network/publicIPAddresses where properties.ipConfiguration is null; surface via policy compliance dashboard and feed a scheduled cleanup workflow.
guardrails.notes: No built-in exists; a small custom definition makes orphan drift visible continuously instead of per-assessment.

guardrails.title: Enforce Log Analytics daily cap and retention limits
guardrails.custom_policy_hint: Audit Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces where properties.workspaceCapping.dailyQuotaGb equals -1 or properties.retentionInDays greater than <your standard, e.g. 90>; optionally Modify to set the cap on non-production workspaces.
guardrails.notes: Caps act as a circuit breaker against runaway log ingestion — the most common silent cost grower.

guardrails.title: Audit VMs/SQL not using Azure Hybrid Benefit
guardrails.custom_policy_hint: Audit Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines where storageProfile.osDisk.osType equals 'Windows' and licenseType not in ('Windows_Server','Windows_Client'); similar audit on Microsoft.Sql resources where licenseType equals 'LicenseIncluded'.
guardrails.notes: Only meaningful if the organization actually holds eligible licenses — confirm with licensing before assigning.

guardrails.title: Require budgets on every subscription
guardrails.notes: Assign at management-group scope so every new subscription gets a budget with action-group alerts automatically. Verify the GUID against your cloud's built-in catalog before assigning (az policy definition list --query "[?contains(displayName,'budget')]").
